subject: CTF estimation failing on many good micrographs
body:
Hello Dimitry,

I have CTF estimation failing on about half of my micrographs despite them having very good thon rings. 
What are the optimal search parameters or which params should I play with to try fix this? 
Also, is there a way to only reprocess theses bad mics and not the whole set? 

My CTF search params were:
Window size: 512pix; Range 24-2.8Å; Defocus range 5-0um; use movie sum unchecked. It is K3 data with 0.8e/A2//frame. 

Best,

Amedee

> On Jul 25, 2020, at 7:06 AM, Dimitry Tegunov <tegunov@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> Weird! Can you come up with a minimal set of Warp's XML metadata (no corresponding image data needed), MDOCs, IMOD alignments and the necessary folder structure to reproduce it, and send it to me?
> 
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
> 
> 
> On Saturday, July 25, 2020 at 1:05:53 AM UTC+2, Benjamin Barad wrote:
> Hi Dimitry,
> 
> I am trying to process some older tilt series data through warp, and running into an interesting error. The data was collected at a period of time when we were saving frames into individual folders for each tilt series, so in order to better match the workflow of warp I moved a couple of tilt series frames to a single folder (out of their subfolders from serialEM). I did the same for the mdoc files for the whole tilt series. I am able to do frame processing, export to imod and perform alignment in etomo, but when trying to bring the alignment files back into warp to make tomostars, I get a pair of the following errors that prevent import: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object."
> 
> Is this because I moved the files around in a non-ideal way? Do I need to preserve the original relative folder structure? Since warp succeeded in everything up to and including exporting to etomo, I am a little surprised to run into these errors this far down the line. Another possible issue is that we have recalibrated the pixel size since these were collected, and I manually updated the pixel size during frame alignment - but again, I would have expected the issue to arise when the rawtlt and st files were made out of the frames and mdoc files.
> 
> I should add that I've gotten newer data (collected to match the folder structure of EMD-10164) through the pipeline successfully, which is why I suspect the issue is one of these two differences with the old data.
> 
> Benjamin Barad, PhD
> Postdoctoral Associate
> Grotjahn Lab @ Scripps Research
> He/His/Him
> 
> --
